verdict_reasoning: SRC is canonically a cytoplasmic Src-family kinase, with myristoylation anchoring it to the inner leaflet of the PM and intracellular membranes. However, documented non-baseline surface pools exist: (1) cancer cells display SRC on the outer leaflet (cancer-ecto-kinase phenotype); (2) cell activation and integrin signaling induce transient outer-leaflet repositioning; (3) SRC cycles between inner and outer leaflet pools during signaling; (4) some reports of regulated exocytosis-mediated surface delivery in specialized contexts. The baseline state is inner-leaflet / cytoplasmic, but multiple cell-state and activation contexts move it to the outer face, qualifying as contextual rather than canonical surface or purely no.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Degree of stability and breadth of SRC outer-leaflet surfacing in cancer vs. activation contexts; whether documented PM cycling + cancer ecto-pool qualifies as `cell_state_induced` or should be gated more narrowly. SRC is well-characterized as inner-leaflet-anchored and cytoplasmic in naive cells, but the extent and functional significance of non-baseline outer-leaflet display remains contextual rather than fully canonical.
